title: Operating Systems
summary: 9618 AS — OS functions, process management, scheduling, memory management, and user interfaces.
learningObjectives: State the main functions of an operating system.
Explain process states and scheduling algorithms.
Describe paging and segmentation for memory management.

simpleExplanation.title: The manager between hardware and software
simpleExplanation.summary: The OS abstracts hardware, schedules CPU time, manages memory, handles I/O, and provides security boundaries between programs.
steps: Process: program in execution — states: ready, running, blocked.
Round-robin: time slices; priority: higher priority runs first.
Virtual memory: pages swapped to disk when RAM full.
Multitasking: rapid context switching gives illusion of parallelism.

sections.content: **Operating systems** (5.1) connect to **CPU architecture** (4.1) and **resource management** questions. Know **FCFS**, **round-robin**, and **priority** scheduling with a small Gantt-style trace.

items: Kernel mode vs user mode — privileged instructions protected
Interrupts signal events (I/O complete, timer tick)
Deadlock: four conditions — mutual exclusion, hold-and-wait, no preemption, circular wait
Device drivers translate OS requests to hardware commands
